pi_items.source: Verified against Wireshark official documentation (https://wiki.wireshark.org/DisplayFilters). This is the correct display filter syntax for TCP reset packets.
Verified against Wireshark official documentation. This filter correctly identifies initial SYN packets without ACK. Tested with tshark and found frames 1 and 9 match this filter.

pi_items.metadata_value: Task assumes RST packets exist in the PCAP
pi_items.source: Analyzed 200722_tcp_anon.pcapng with tshark: examined all 35 packets and found ZERO packets with tcp.flags.reset == 1. Only SYN, SYN-ACK, ACK, PSH-ACK, and FIN-ACK packets are present. No RST packets exist.

pi_items.metadata_value: Task asks to identify first packet highlighted by RST rule, but first SYN packet is at frame 1
pi_items.source: Verified with tshark -r 200722_tcp_anon.pcapng -Y 'tcp.flags.syn == 1 && tcp.flags.ack == 0' returns frame 1. This is the first SYN packet in the capture.
Counted with tshark: 35 total packets in 200722_tcp_anon.pcapng

pi_items.metadata_value: Red (RGB: 255, 0, 0) maps to [65535, 0, 0] in 16-bit Wireshark format
pi_items.source: Wireshark coloringrules uses 16-bit RGB values (0-65535). Conversion: 8-bit 255 = 16-bit 65535. Verified in verifier.py line 67 regex pattern.

pi_items.metadata_value: White (RGB: 255, 255, 255) maps to [65535, 65535, 65535] in 16-bit Wireshark format
pi_items.source: Wireshark 16-bit color format. White at full intensity is 65535 for all channels.

pi_items.metadata_value: Yellow (RGB: 255, 255, 0) maps to [65535, 65535, 0] in 16-bit Wireshark format
pi_items.source: Wireshark coloringrules uses 16-bit RGB. Verified in verifier.py line 90 regex pattern.

pi_items.metadata_value: Black (RGB: 0, 0, 0) maps to [0, 0, 0] in 16-bit Wireshark format
pi_items.source: Wireshark 16-bit color format. Black is 0 for all channels.

pi_items.metadata_value: Not explicitly stated but verifier.py line 51 indicates format

privileged_info_summary: The PCAP file 200722_tcp_anon.pcapng is a real, publicly available Wireshark sample capture (13 KB, 35 packets) from https://wiki.wireshark.org/uploads/__moin_import__/attachments/SampleCaptures/200722_tcp_anon.pcapng. The filter syntax 'tcp.flags.reset == 1' and 'tcp.flags.syn == 1 && tcp.flags.ack == 0' are both correct Wireshark display filters. However, there is a critical discrepancy: the PCAP file contains ZERO RST packets. All 35 packets are SYN, SYN-ACK, ACK, PSH-ACK, or FIN-ACK packets. The first SYN packet (matching the SYN filter without ACK) is correctly at frame 1. Wireshark uses 16-bit RGB color values (0-65535), so the specified colors are correct: Red=[65535,0,0], White=[65535,65535,65535], Yellow=[65535,65535,0], Black=[0,0,0]. The coloringrules file format is @Name@Filter@[bg_r,bg_g,bg_b]@[fg_r,fg_g,fg_b].
